articles.title: Terror suspects indicted after allegedly throwing bombs at NYC protest outside mayor's mansion
articles.summary: The pair of terror suspects accused of trying to bomb a protest outside New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's residence in an ISIS-inspired attack have been indicted.

articles.title: A-10 Warthog given new maritime role targeting boats in Iran after efforts to retire aircraft
articles.summary: The A-10 Warthog has a new maritime role in targeting boats in the war in Iran that could extend the life of the aircraft after the U.S. Air Force had been attempting to retire it.

articles.title: Abortion pill mifepristone stays available by mail for now as FDA faces 6-month review deadline
articles.summary: A federal judge ruled mifepristone can still be mailed nationwide for now but warned the abortion pill's FDA guidelines could face major changes soon.

articles.title: Pope Leo calls out Trump’s Iran rhetoric before last-minute ceasefire emerges
articles.summary: Pope Leo called President Trump's threat against Iran 'truly unacceptable,' urging world leaders to return to the negotiating table and reject war always.

articles.title: Gary Woodland savors Masters return after brain surgery, PTSD battle nearly ended his career
articles.summary: Gary Woodland opens up about battling PTSD at the Masters, saying large galleries at Augusta National trigger fears despite heavy security presence.

articles.title: Tori Spelling speaks out after car crash that sent her and her kids to the hospital
articles.summary: Tori Spelling and four of her children were taken to a hospital after a car crash in Temecula. She shared a grateful update on their recovery.

articles.title: Patriots coach Mike Vrabel responds after photos with New York Times NFL reporter leak
articles.summary: Mike Vrabel addresses photos showing him with reporter Dianna Russini at a Sedona resort, calling the interaction innocent and any suggestion otherwise laughable.

articles.title: California advocates insist on raising minimum wage to $30 despite research showing 'negative outcomes'
